The focus on lifelong learning is expanding the education technology (EdTech) industry beyond traditional education. This includes growing demand from adult learners and corporate training. The adoption of AI-driven personalized learning platforms is accelerating the market growth. These platforms enable customized learning experiences based on individual student needs. AI integration enhances learning outcomes by providing adaptive content and real-time feedback. ![Image 1: Education technology market size and growth forecast (2023-2033)](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/static/img/research/education-technology-market-size.webp) Educational institutions are increasingly adopting these solutions to enhance engagement and efficiency. The demand for data-driven insights is driving wider adoption among educators. This shift is strengthening the move toward intelligent and scalable digital learning systems. For instance, in January 2026, Fermi AI PTE. LTD., an AI-based EdTech company in Singapore, launched an AI-based learning platform in the U.S. and India, focusing on STEM subjects and personalized learning. The Nimitz-class aircraft carrier is one of the largest military ships in the world and the flagship of the the US Navy\u2019s Carrier Strike Group Three, which also includes three Arleigh Burke class destroyers. The aircraft assigned to fly from the USS Abraham Lincoln are part of Carrier Air Wing Nine, made up of eight squadrons. Among the aircraft designed to operate from its flight deck include the F-35 Lighting II and F-18 Super Hornet fighter jets. The ship\u2019s nuclear power system means it can stay at sea for long periods of time without needing to call into port, as it can be restocked with supplies by support vessels. ![Image 4: A graphic detailing key features of USS Abraham Lincoln and also overlaying its silhouette with that of the Eiffel Twoer laid on its side - both are about the same size](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/640/cpsprodpb/vivo/live/images/2026/2/16/8f26771d-6dd9-4671-8990-24b3d6b0eb5a.png.webp)  3.   ### Is the UK planning the biggest increase in defence spending since the Cold War?published at 16:44 GMT 16 February published at 16:44 16 February ![Image 5](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/ace/standard/128/cpsprodpb//vivo/live/images/2025/6/3/72725dd0-f120-4e12-a924-37d73d721a0f.jpg.webp)**Anthony Reuben** BBC Verify senior journalist UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper was asked by Laura Kuenssberg yesterday about the whether the government would be speeding up plans to increase defence spending. Our team has plans to roll out more products internationally this year including guest marketing,  restaurant retail, Toast Tables, as well as our hotel PMS integrations. With 2,000 live locations as of Q2  we are excited about our progress and continue to remain bullish on the long term potential given how  early we are in the international opportunity.  Next, our third priority is expanding our addressable market into new adjacencies including Enterprise  and Food & Beverage Retail.  We recently expanded our partnership with Wetzel \u02bcs Pretzels by signing an extension for another 100  stores in addition to the 300 locations already on our platform. We continue to roll-out Toast across a  number of marquee brands, including Wetzel \u02bcs and Barbeque Holdings which are part of fast growing  MTY brands. And the improvements we have made to our platform combined with a strong pipeline  gives us confidence in steadily increasing enterprise penetration over time.  3As I mentioned in our investor day in May, the work we have put in over the past decade building out  our platform has allowed us to enter new verticals including grocery, convenience stores, and bottle  shops. There are 220,000 locations and $660 billion in spend in these markets in the US alone - so far,  we \u02bcve booked 1,000 new customers. We see a significant growth opportunity here in part because so  much of this market is still using legacy on-prem solutions and see the benefits of our integrated cloud  platform.  For example, Big Tree Hospitality Group in Portland, Maine recently launched three markets on Toast  in addition to their portfolio of award-winning restaurants that have been with us since 2016. Since  adding Toast, their sta ff  has been able to o ff er better service to their guests by making back o ff ice tasks  including inventory management much more streamlined. We see stories like this across many of our  early retail customers, and have confidence that we can drive significant growth in this segment.  Finally, our fourth priority is to deliver operating leverage in our core business as we scale to both drive  shareholder returns and have the capital available to invest into our nascent market segments. Our  Adjusted EBITDA was $92 million in the second quarter, a $77 million improvement from a year ago. I  am very proud of the team \u02bcs ability to both drive strong growth and this level of margin expansion in a  short period of time. As we look to the second half of the year, we will invest more back into our  business to support our growth plans while working towards our long term margin goals.  To wrap up, I am confident in how we \u02bcre executing. We are still in the early innings and are prepared to \n...\nexpectations.
